7.reason: [The condition for an empty stack should be `top == -1`, not `top == 0`. This causes pop operations to incorrectly report "error" even when the stack has one element (at index 0).]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The condition for an empty stack should be `top == -1`, not `top == 0`. This causes pop operations to incorrectly report "error" even when the stack has one element (at index 0).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The condition should be `top == -1` to check if the stack is empty, not `top == 0`. When top is 0, there's one element in the stack, so popping should be allowed.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [The condition for an empty stack should be `top == -1`, not `top == 0`. This causes pop operations to incorrectly report "error" even when the stack has one element (at index 0).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [The condition should be `top == -1` to check if the stack is empty, not `top == 0`. When `top == -1`, the stack is empty; when `top == 0`, there's one element.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [The condition for an empty stack should be `top == -1`, not `top == 0`. This causes pop operations on a stack with one element (top=0) to be incorrectly allowed, and pop on empty stack (top=-1) to incorrectly trigger "error".]

13.reason: [The loop condition `scanf(...)` returns the number of successfully read items. It should be compared to `!= EOF` or `== 1` to correctly detect end-of-input. As written, it may cause infinite loops or undefined behavior on input failure.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The loop condition `scanf(...)` returns the number of successfully read items. It should be compared to `!= EOF` or `== 1` to correctly detect end-of-input. As written, it may cause infinite loops or undefined behavior on input failure.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The loop condition should check for `!= EOF` to properly detect end-of-input. As written, it may result in an infinite loop or undefined behavior on some systems when input ends.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [The loop condition `while(scanf("%d",&a))` only breaks on input failure or EOF if scanf returns 0 or negative. However, scanf returns the number of successfully read items, so it returns 1 on success and continues indefinitely until non-integer input. It should check for `!= EOF` or `== 1` explicitly to match the reference behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [The loop condition should check for `!= EOF` to properly detect end-of-input. As written, it may result in an infinite loop or undefined behavior on input failure.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [The loop condition should check for `!= EOF` to properly detect end-of-input. Using just `scanf(...)` may lead to infinite loop or undefined behavior when input ends, because `scanf` returns EOF (typically -1) which is non-zero and treated as true.]
